notes: Gold DQ rules for Anthropic-sourced columns added to consumable.ai_exposure via LEFT JOIN from base.anthropic_observed_exposure. Separate file (parallels gold-futureproof-engine-backfill-ai.json pattern) because the base gold-ai-exposure.json is already signed off and this spec is an additive schema evolution. Rules cover: Anthropic-column population where expected, internal consistency of Anthropic-sourced fields, and regression-safety against existing stat_res / boss_ai_score. observed_exposure_pct population threshold is >=60% (SOC-coverage-limited), revised from spec's 70% per EDA finding. automation_pct and augmentation_pct carry through from Silver in 0-100 percent units (consistent with observed_exposure_pct and every other _pct field in the project). GLD-AIE-ANT-002 and GLD-AIE-ANT-005 were revised 2026-04-17 after DQ execution: ANT-005 corrected from 0-1 fraction-range to 0-100 percent-range (unit contract fix); ANT-002 threshold lowered from 100% to >=85% to account for the ~15% of SOCs with all tasks fully filtered (automation_pct legitimately NULL per SLV-AOE-010 semantics) while observed_exposure_pct stays populated from sum-aggregation. See governance/audit-trail/2026-04-17-dq-rule-writer-anthropic-economic-index-post-execution.md for rationale.

rules.name: observed_exposure_pct populated: >= 60% of rows
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN coverage < 0.60 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN observed_exposure_pct IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS coverage FROM consumable.ai_exposure)
rules.description: At least 60% of consumable.ai_exposure rows must have observed_exposure_pct populated after the LEFT JOIN against base.anthropic_observed_exposure. Coverage is bounded by SOC overlap: Anthropic covers 510 of 832 occupations. Below 60% means the JOIN is degraded (bad key, SOC-format mismatch, or Anthropic Silver row loss).
rules.rationale: EDA: 'SOC codes matching (target intersect source): 510 / 832 = 61.30%.' EDA: 'Revise Silver P0 threshold from >=80% to >=60%.' This Gold rule propagates the same 60% floor. Revised from spec's Gold DQ '>=70%' because 70% is unreachable given 61.3% SOC overlap. Threshold: coverage >= 0.60.
rules.threshold_divergence_note: Diverged from spec's '>=70%' based on EDA finding that Anthropic SOC coverage against consumable.occupation_profiles (the driver of consumable.ai_exposure population) is 61.3%. Even a perfect LEFT JOIN cannot exceed this. Revised to >=60% to match the Silver SOC-match threshold.

rules.name: automation_pct populated where observed_exposure_pct present: >= 85%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN populated_rate < 0.85 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN automation_pct IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS populated_rate FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE observed_exposure_pct IS NOT NULL)
rules.description: Among rows where observed_exposure_pct is populated, at least 85% must also have automation_pct populated. This is NOT 100% because SOCs whose contributing Bronze tasks were all fully filtered (filtered >= 0.999) produce NULL automation_pct by the ingestor's _collapse_automation logic while still contributing to observed_exposure_pct via sum-aggregation. DQ execution 2026-04-17 observed 77 / 520 = ~14.8% null automation_pct on populated rows -- this is correct behavior, not a defect. Threshold >=85% gives ~3 points of headroom above the observed ~85.2% populated rate; a drop below 85% would indicate the JOIN is actually dropping rows or a future release has pushed more SOCs into the all-filtered bucket.
rules.rationale: Revised 2026-04-17 after DQ execution. Original threshold (100%) assumed observed_exposure_pct and automation_pct were always populated together; this was wrong. Spec implementation note 6: 'When filtered >= 0.999, both fields emit None.' EDA: '31.69% of tasks are fully filtered.' At the SOC level, ~15% of SOCs end up with every contributing task filtered -- observed_exposure_pct is still defined (sum of task shares), but automation_pct is legitimately NULL. Domain context edge case (SOC all-filtered): 'Correct behavior: observed_exposure_pct > 0, automation_pct NULL.' Observed on 2026-04-17 run: 77 / 520 = 14.8% NULL rate. Threshold: populated_rate >= 0.85.
rules.revision_note: Threshold lowered from 100% to >=85%. Original assumption (both fields populated together) was wrong: SOCs with all-filtered contributing tasks correctly produce populated observed_exposure_pct with NULL automation_pct. Observed rate ~85.2% populated.

rules.name: anthropic_source_release non-null where Anthropic fields present: 100%
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE (observed_exposure_pct IS NOT NULL OR automation_pct IS NOT NULL OR anthropic_task_count IS NOT NULL) AND anthropic_source_release IS NULL
rules.description: If any Anthropic-sourced column (observed_exposure_pct, automation_pct, anthropic_task_count) is populated, anthropic_source_release must also be populated. This enforces provenance integrity: every row with Anthropic data carries the release stamp.
rules.rationale: Spec Gold schema: anthropic_source_release required=no (nullable because row may lack Anthropic coverage). But when present, it anchors provenance. Domain context: 'Release pinning required ... pipeline is pinned to 2025-03-27.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: observed_exposure_pct range where populated: 0 <= x <= 100
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE observed_exposure_pct IS NOT NULL AND (observed_exposure_pct < 0 OR observed_exposure_pct > 100)
rules.description: observed_exposure_pct carries through from Silver in 0-100 percent units. A value outside [0, 100] indicates a Gold transformation corrupted the column or the Silver input was out of range.
rules.rationale: Propagates SLV-AOE-002 to Gold. EDA: Silver observed_exposure_pct max 7.51. Threshold: 0 out-of-range values.

rules.name: automation_pct range where populated: 0 <= x <= 100 (percent units)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE automation_pct IS NOT NULL AND (automation_pct < 0 OR automation_pct > 100)
rules.description: automation_pct carries through from Silver in 0-100 percent units, consistent with observed_exposure_pct and every other _pct field in this project. A value outside [0, 100] indicates a Gold transform mis-scaled the column or the Silver input was corrupt. Revised 2026-04-17 after DQ execution revealed an original 0-1 fraction-range threshold was wrong for the as-built schema (392 FAILs against rows like 11-1011 Chief Executives automation_pct=24.31, which is a correct 0-100 percent value).
rules.rationale: Propagates SLV-AOE-011 to Gold. Ingestor emits automation_pct in percent units (directive + feedback_loop) * 100; Silver volume-weighted mean preserves the 0-100 range. Threshold: 0 violations.
rules.revision_note: Unit contract fix: threshold was 0-1 fraction, revised to 0-100 percent to match as-built ingestor output and Bronze/Silver/Gold _pct convention.

rules.name: anthropic_task_count >= 1 where populated
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE anthropic_task_count IS NOT NULL AND anthropic_task_count < 1
rules.description: Where anthropic_task_count is populated, it must be >= 1 (a row with 0 contributing tasks shouldn't exist in Silver, let alone Gold). A zero/negative value indicates the Silver aggregation produced an empty-group row that leaked through.
rules.rationale: Propagates SLV-AOE-005 to Gold. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Regression: existing stat_res / boss_ai_score unchanged by Anthropic addition
rules.sql: WITH current AS (SELECT soc_code, stat_res, boss_ai_score FROM consumable.ai_exposure), baseline AS (SELECT soc_code, stat_res, boss_ai_score FROM consumable.ai_exposure_baseline_pre_anthropic) SELECT c.soc_code, c.stat_res AS current_stat_res, b.stat_res AS baseline_stat_res, c.boss_ai_score AS current_boss_ai, b.boss_ai_score AS baseline_boss_ai FROM current c INNER JOIN baseline b ON c.soc_code = b.soc_code WHERE (c.stat_res IS DISTINCT FROM b.stat_res) OR (c.boss_ai_score IS DISTINCT FROM b.boss_ai_score)
rules.description: Adding Anthropic columns to consumable.ai_exposure via LEFT JOIN must NOT modify existing stat_res or boss_ai_score values. This regression rule compares current rows to a pre-Anthropic baseline (consumable.ai_exposure_baseline_pre_anthropic -- a snapshot table taken before this spec landed). Any divergence means the Gold transformer accidentally mutated pre-existing columns. Rule is P2 because it requires an explicit baseline snapshot; if the snapshot doesn't exist the rule is inapplicable and should be archived.
rules.rationale: Spec Gold DQ: 'No change to existing stat_res/boss_ai_score: Regression test.' A LEFT JOIN by design shouldn't alter existing columns, but a buggy transformer could re-derive or overwrite them. Baseline-snapshot-compare is the standard regression pattern. Threshold: 0 divergent rows. If consumable.ai_exposure_baseline_pre_anthropic is unavailable at runtime, @dq-engineer should create it from the pre-change Iceberg snapshot or archive this rule.

rules.name: anthropic_source_release pinned to release_2025_03_27
rules.sql: SELECT DISTINCT anthropic_source_release FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE anthropic_source_release IS NOT NULL AND anthropic_source_release <> 'release_2025_03_27'
rules.description: All non-null anthropic_source_release values must equal the pinned release 'release_2025_03_27'. Any other value indicates the pipeline pulled from an unexpected release.
rules.rationale: Propagates SLV-AOE-012 to Gold. Domain context: 'Hard-pin release_2025_03_27.' P2 (not P1) at Gold because it is double-guarded by the Silver rule; Gold-level check catches only a transformer-injection bug. Threshold: 0 distinct mismatches.
